Service identification.content: Service TAM APM Dynatrace for Argenta Unique ID PRD.0.L.135 Service Model Technical Application Management (TAM) This service is dependant of other layers (IaaS and/or PaaS) which are indicated in the ‘Obligatory’ chapter of this document and which have their own Service Description. The combination of the layers assures the customer of the ‘infrastructure up to the technical application’ management. These obligatority services are called the ‘Platform’.

Technical Application Management.content: Application Performance Management (Observability) is a Service which foresees in the monitoring of the performance of specific business (critical) applications and its end2end flows. This service is based on the Dynatrace Software Intelligence Platform. The customer can choose out of a range of modular components within Dynatrace which can build up to a full scope APM service. The Service provides the tasks and activities needed to manage an Application Performance Management environment using Dynatrace (The Application) from a technical perspective. Together with the underlaying obligatory platform services, Cegeka unburdens the customer with all technical aspects from infrastructure up to the technical Application. The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters.

The Application.content: The Dynatrace solution consists out of the following components:  Below a graphical overview of a typical set-up:  The Dynatrace solution is hosted within the Cegeka datacenters in Hasselt & Geleen and has a permanent uplink with Dynatrace Mission Control in Ireland.  Dynatrace Mission Control provides fully automated management capabilities in a proactive way that keep Dynatrace server secure, reliable, and up-to-date—all while saving the user from the hassles of administrative tasks like upgrades and troubleshooting. Dynatrace product experts can remotely assist with upgrades and troubleshooting when you run into problems.

Standard Service features.content: Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the Dynatrace technology in order to perform the Service. Cegeka advises in the setup of the underlaying platform in order to set it up according to the technology requirements of the Application. This may be different for each Customer environment (DTAP - Development, Test, Acceptance, Production and it’s variants). High availability setup: this setup advise includes also the setup of HA (High Available) environments where needed. Cegeka will advise a cost effective solution within the context  of the available platform(s) of the Customer and the best practices of the Application: Cegeka performs and maintains the technical installation of the Application on the agreed platform ensuring its correct working. Technical setup is documented on operational level and is available to the Customer. Disaster Recovery Disaster Recovery is available for the Dynatrace Managed platform and is based on two mechanisms:  User group meeting (operational meeting) Customer foresees a recurrent user group meeting where Cegeka amongst others will: Dynatrace Customer Success meetings (operational meeting) Cegeka will join the recurrent Argenta & Dynatrace Customer success meetings. Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Application. Automation is applied where possible. Proactive management Housekeeping includes following regular checks: Patching See Life Cycle Management. Dynatrace is continuously updated. Patching of the underlying platform is part of the Service of that platform. Dynatrace follows an agile development process with sprints every two weeks where every 2 nd sprint is publicly released. These releases are then supported for: This frequent release process demands continuous lifecycle management where updates will be deployed at the following frequency: Cegeka is fully responsible for the patch process and all patching activities will follow the change management procedures in place. Life Cycle Management Cegeka monitors the Technology version of the service and notifies the Customer in accordance with the agreed LCM policy (see cross-functional Services) when used versions are subject to end-of-support or end-of-life. Appropriate actions (updates, replacement or other actions) are planned in accordance with agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis in case of major updates. Patches and minor updates are included in the service cost as part of ‘Patching’.  Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Application on all Customer environments. Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service and are based on evaluation of incidents, problem management or other suggestions. The underlaying platform is also monitored according to the related Service Description of the platform. Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management. Also, a specific Dynatrace monitoring profile to monitor the availability of the platform itself, this profile includes: Management of technical users The management of technical users (for engineers) is done by Cegeka in agreement with the Customer and in accordance to the Customer IAM policies. Security Management The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders. SIEM Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request. Underlaying Platform hardening The underlaying platform and OS will be hardened as part of and according to the Service Description of the related Services. The operational Backup Plan contains the information on frequency and retention of the backups in accordance with the Customer backup policy. Restores can be done both on file and on full system level. These can be requested via the regular RFC process. Duration of a restore is dependant of the request (file or full; size of the item(s) to be restored). Application settings are subject to the backup of the underlaying platform. All the important Dynatrace Managed configurations and monitoring data will be backupped. For the avoidance of doubt, the following items are excluded from backup: Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals). Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals). If an incident cannot be resolved by Cegeka’s engineering support, due to for example a bug in the software, Cegeka will make use of the Argenta support contract and log a case with Dynatrace. Resolution times of incidents where support is needed from Dynatrace are subject to the selected support contract. The customer is responsible for defining the monitoring needs and specifying how the application should be monitored.

Optional Service features.content: This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features. The following optional features are possible with the Service.

Requests.content: Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). request type Description Lead time Service Requests Deployment of OneAgent 2 working days Add/remove a key service request 2 working days Add/remove a key user action 2 working days Add/remove AKS cluster to Dynatrace monitoring 2 working days Add/modify/remove a synthetic HTTP monitor (without pre/post-execution scripts) 2 working days Add/modify/remove a synthetic single-URL browser monitor 2 working days Normal Changes* Any other request. Examples: Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request. *are not obliged to follow the change management process as they can be executed via the New Delivery Model following a feature.

It is the customers responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Service delivery location.content: This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) Office environment.

Service Reporting.content: The following reports are provided via a dashboard in Dynatrace:

Data classification.content: Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service. Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service. In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes). The Dynatrace solution is hosted within the Cegeka datacenters in Hasselt & Geleen and has a permanent uplink with Dynatrace Mission Control in Ireland.  Dynatrace Mission Control provides fully automated management capabilities in a proactive way that keep Dynatrace server secure, reliable, and up-to-date—all while saving the user from the hassles of administrative tasks like upgrades and troubleshooting. Dynatrace product experts can remotely assist with upgrades and troubleshooting when you run into problems.

Adherence to Cross-Functional Services.content: The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in other sections of the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services Anti-Malware management This service is not subject to Anti-Malware management. Anti-Malware management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. Vulnerability management & compliancy management (hardening) This service is not subject to vulnerability & compliancy management. Vulnerability & compliancy management is done on the underlaying OS management layer. The Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.

Service Window.content: The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.

Service Level.content: Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. Availability The following Availability Performance Metrics are provided for the Service: KPI ID KPI name Availability Service Window Metric KPI.0.135.Gold TAM Gold Gold % availability in Service Window The availability measurement is monthly and calculated as: (Total time of service window of the measured period – time of outage based on Cegeka infrastructure monitoring during the measured period) / Total time of service window of the measured period) * 100

Standard Service Billing.content: The service is billed on a monthly basis. ID Component Billing element BILLING UNIT SKU.0.L.135.L01 APM Dynatrace TAM service base fee Per Customer SKU.0.L.135.L03 APM Dynatrace TAM service Application NON-PRD Per App NON-PRD SKU.0.L.135.L07 APM Dynatrace TAM service application PRD Per App PRD  Measurement The quantity of each billing element is determined on a monthly basis: Calculation / Billing The price(s) is/are determined in the pricing schedule where this Service is residing. The Service is billed per billing element:

Change Request Billing.content: Changes are billed on a monthly basis. Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact. Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.
